mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high UNRATE periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPI) and federal funds rates signal monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-moving CPI/FEDFUNDS up periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust demand for goods and services, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high HOUST periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in INDPRO up periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates deteriorating demand conditions that lower corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low RSAFS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) signal a tightening labor market that increases corporate wage pressures and drives inflation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion that raises corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 up periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.
